auth_notes: Most enterprise DMS platforms (like M-Files or LogicalDOC) require an initial POST request to an authentication endpoint to exchange credentials for a Session Token or SID. This token must then be included in the headers (e.g., 'X-Authentication' or 'Authorization') for all subsequent calls.
rate_limits: Typically governed by the host server configuration. Cloud-hosted instances often implement a limit of 100-200 requests per minute per user session.
gotchas: Binary data handling: Uploading documents requires the HTTP Request node to be set to 'Multipart-form-data' with specific property names for the file buffer.
Session Expiry: Tokens are often short-lived, requiring a 'Try/Catch' or 'Error Trigger' logic to re-authenticate if a 401 Unauthorized error occurs.
Object ID Mapping: Most DMS APIs use internal GUIDs or Integer IDs for folders and document types rather than names, requiring lookup steps.
research_notes: Integration will require a three-stage workflow: 1. Auth node (HTTP Request) to get the session token. 2. Logic node to extract and set the token as a global variable. 3. Functional HTTP Request nodes using the 'Send Binary Data' option for document transfers. Polling will be necessary for 'Watch' functionality as native webhooks are rarely supported in standard DMS configurations.
